findings.evidence: Existing general APIs are `GridState.pointSet_eq_insert_insert_inter_swapColumns` at `code/TauCeti/KnotTheory/Grid/Diagram.lean:358` and `GridState.swapColumns_pointSet_eq_insert_insert_inter` at `:383`; `RectangleSwap.lean:69` proves `R.target_eq_swapColumns`, while this PR specializes directly at `GradingChange.lean:88`.
findings.fix: First add a general theorem for `x : GridState n`, `{a b : Fin n}`, `h : a ≠ b`, e.g. `GridPoint.J x.pointSet s - GridPoint.J (x.swapColumns a b).pointSet s = GridPoint.J {(a, x a)} s + GridPoint.J {(b, x b)} s - GridPoint.J {(a, x b)} s - GridPoint.J {(b, x a)} s`, then derive the rectangle theorem by rewriting with `R.target_eq_swapColumns`, `R.bottom`, and `R.top`.
findings.issue: `GridRectangleBetween.J_source_sub_J_target` proves a column-swap `J`-change formula only for `R : GridRectangleBetween x y`, even though the rectangle proof uses only that `y = x.swapColumns R.left R.right` and the existing arbitrary swap point-set decompositions.

summary: The Alexander-difference identity is at the right generality, but the rectangle `J` change is an avoidable specialization of the existing arbitrary column-swap API.
